<!-- Code generated from the comments of the cpuFlagsConfig struct in builder/proxmox/common/config.go; DO NOT EDIT MANUALLY -->

- `aes` (string) - Activate AES instruction set for HW acceleration.
  Can be `"on"`, `"off"`, or unset.

- `amd_no_ssb` (string) - Notifies guest OS that host is not vulnerable for Spectre on AMD CPUs.
  Can be `"on"`, `"off"`, or unset.

- `amd_ssbd` (string) - Improves Spectre mitigation performance with AMD CPUs, best used with `virt_ssbd`.
  Can be `"on"`, `"off"`, or unset.

- `hv_evmcs` (string) - Improve performance for nested virtualization. Only supported on Intel CPUs.
  Can be `"on"`, `"off"`, or unset.

- `hv_tlb_flush` (string) - Improve performance in overcommitted Windows guests. May lead to guest bluescreens on old CPUs.
  Can be `"on"`, `"off"`, or unset.

- `ibpb` (string) - Allows improved Spectre mitigation with AMD CPUs.
  Can be `"on"`, `"off"`, or unset.

- `md_clear` (string) - Required to let the guest OS know if MDS is mitigated correctly.
  Can be `"on"`, `"off"`, or unset.

- `pcid` (string) - Meltdown fix cost reduction on Westmere, Sandy-, and IvyBridge Intel CPUs.
  Can be `"on"`, `"off"`, or unset.

- `pdpe1gb` (string) - Allow guest OS to use 1GB size pages, if host HW supports it.
  Can be `"on"`, `"off"`, or unset.

- `ssbd` (string) - Protection for "Speculative Store Bypass" for Intel models.
  Can be `"on"`, `"off"`, or unset.

- `spec_ctrl` (string) - Allows improved Spectre mitigation with Intel CPUs.
  Can be `"on"`, `"off"`, or unset.

- `virt_ssbd` (string) - Basis for "Speculative Store Bypass" protection for AMD models.
  Can be `"on"`, `"off"`, or unset.

- `nested_virt` (string) - Enable nested virtualization. Allows the guest VM to run hypervisors itself.
  Requires nested KVM support to be enabled on the Proxmox host (e.g.,
  `options kvm-intel nested=1` in `/etc/modprobe.d/kvm-intel.conf`).
  Can be `"on"`, `"off"`, or unset.

<!-- End of code generated from the comments of the cpuFlagsConfig struct in builder/proxmox/common/config.go; -->
